title: Research
text:
My research focuses on applying advanced biostatistical, epidemiological, and artificial intelligence methods to clinical and public health problems.




Regression modeling, Bayesian methods, multilevel models, survival analysis, and clinical prediction models.



Design and analysis of clinical and observational studies, disease risk modeling, and evidence generation for healthcare decision-making.



Maternal and child health, malaria burden, infectious diseases, non-communicable diseases, and health service utilization.



Machine learning, interpretable AI, federated learning, privacy-preserving analytics, and AI-supported clinical research.



Use of R, Quarto, GitHub, and open science tools for transparent and reproducible health research.




My current work emphasizes Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling, malaria burden estimation, maternal and newborn health analytics, and federated learning for privacy-preserving healthcare research.

title: Lab Vision
text:
The long-term vision is to establish a research and training laboratory at Ambo University, Ethiopia focused on biostatistics, clinical epidemiology, public health analytics, and artificial intelligence for health.


To strengthen data-driven health research through statistical innovation, epidemiological methods, reproducible science, and responsible artificial intelligence.




Training students and researchers in biostatistics and epidemiology
Supporting clinical and public health research design
Developing reproducible R and Quarto workflows
Applying AI and machine learning to health data
Promoting collaborative research in Ethiopia and Africa




The lab will support research, mentorship, consultancy, short courses, workshops, and collaborative projects in public health and biomedical sciences.

title: About
text:
PhD Candidate in Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Public Health
University of Padova, Italy
Lecturer in Statistics
Ambo University, Ethiopia

I am a biostatistician, epidemiologist, researcher, and academic lecturer with expertise in statistical modeling, public health analytics, clinical epidemiology, and artificial intelligence for health research.
My academic and professional journey spans more than a decade in teaching, research, consultancy, and interdisciplinary collaboration. Since 2013, I have been teaching statistical science courses in Ethiopian universities while actively participating in research and capacity-building activities.
My current research focuses on:

Bayesian statistical modeling
Spatial and spatiotemporal epidemiology
Machine learning and federated learning
Clinical prediction modeling
Maternal and child health
Malaria burden modeling
Artificial intelligence in healthcare
Reproducible research using R and Quarto

I am currently pursuing a PhD in Biostatistics, Epidemiology, and Public Health at the University of Padova, where my work explores innovative methodologies for clinical and public health research using advanced statistical and AI-driven approaches.



My long-term vision is to establish a strong research and training laboratory in Ethiopia focused on:

Biostatistics
Clinical epidemiology
Public health analytics
Artificial intelligence for healthcare
Open science and reproducible research

The laboratory aims to support students, researchers, clinicians, and institutions through training, collaborative research, and methodological innovation.




PhD Candidate in Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Public Health — University of Padova
MSc in Statistics (Biostatistics) — Addis Ababa University
BSc in Statistics — Mekelle University







Lecturer in Statistics
Research advisor
Academic and research collaboration leadership

Ethiopian Statistical Association
International Biometric Society
Computational Sciences and Engineering Society of Ethiopia




I have actively participated in international conferences and workshops related to:

Artificial intelligence in biomedical research
Federated learning
Big data in public health
Statistical modeling
Clinical epidemiology




I believe that advanced statistical science and artificial intelligence should be translated into practical healthcare solutions that improve public health systems, especially in low- and middle-income countries.

title: Jiregna Olani Kedida
text:
I am a PhD candidate in Biostatistics, Epidemiology, and Public Health at the University of Padova and a Lecturer in Statistics at Ambo University, Ethiopia. My work focuses on statistical modeling, epidemiological methods, machine learning, federated learning, and data-driven public health research.

title: Jiregna Olani Kedida
section: Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Public Health AI Lab
text:
Biostatistics, Epidemiology & Public Health AI Lab
The long-term vision is to build a research and training platform that supports students, researchers, clinicians, and public health professionals in Ethiopia and beyond.

title: Jiregna Olani Kedida
text:
Research Focus


Biostatistics
Statistical modeling, regression methods, Bayesian analysis, and clinical prediction models.


Clinical Epidemiology
Maternal and child health, disease burden, survival analysis, and public health evidence.


Public Health AI
Machine learning, federated learning, reproducible research, and AI for health systems.

title: Jiregna Olani Kedida
text:
Current Academic Direction
My current research interests include Bayesian spatiotemporal modeling, malaria burden estimation, maternal and newborn health analytics, interpretable machine learning, and privacy-preserving federated learning.
